title: Blechnum humile
intro: Blechnum humile, also known as the dwarf chain fern, is a small to medium-sized, evergreen fern native to the subtropical regions of Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Bioactive constituents of Blechnum humile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Blechnum humile is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs in tonics and tinctures to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
taxonomy: Blechnum humile is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It is a type of delicate, terrestrial fern that belongs to the order Polypodiales. The genus Blechnum is a group of ferns commonly known as water ferns or chain ferns. Blechnum humile is native to New Zealand and is often found growing in moist, shaded areas. The classification of Blechnum humile also includes its kingdom as Plantae, a group of primarily autotrophic organisms.
